  • Tutorial: Savona Greenhouse with Walls

Tutorial: Savona Greenhouse with Walls

The Savona greenhouse (Boccalatte et al. 2021) is a 5×5 m mini greenhouse with an asymmetric roof (30°/60°). For small buildings, wall heat losses dominate over roof losses, this tutorial shows how to include them.

Geometry

         ┌── ridge (4.17 m)
        /│\
  30°  / │ \  60°
      /  │  \
     /   │   \
────/────┘────\────  gutter (2.0 m)
   0    2.89   5.0   x [m]
ParameterValue
Floor5 × 5 = 25 m²
Mean height3.08 m
Volume77.1 m³
Roof area34.1 m²
Wall area50.8 m² (60% of envelope!)
Total envelope85.0 m²

Using SavonaParams

using GreenhousesJL
using OrdinaryDiffEqTsit5

tmy = load_tmy("Greenhouses/Resources/Data/10Dec-22Nov.txt")

# With walls (default)
p = SavonaParams(tmy; with_walls=true, Kp=200.0, Ti=600.0, T_sp=291.15, Q_max=5000.0)

# Without walls (for comparison)
p_no_walls = SavonaParams(tmy; with_walls=false, Kp=200.0, Ti=600.0, T_sp=291.15, Q_max=5000.0)

SavonaParams automatically computes:

  • Roof area from floor dimensions
  • Wall area: 2 × L × h_gutter + 2 × (W × h_gutter + ½ × W × rise)
  • Thermal capacities from glass properties (1 mm, ρ=2600, c_p=840)
  • Sky view factor for walls: FF_wall_sky = 0.5

Simulation and comparison

u0 = initial_state(p; T0=278.15, T_soil0=276.15)
sol = solve(ODEProblem(greenhouse_rhs!, u0, (0.0, 7*86400.0), p), Tsit5(); reltol=1e-6, saveat=3600.0)

u0_nw = initial_state(p_no_walls; T0=278.15, T_soil0=276.15)
sol_nw = solve(ODEProblem(greenhouse_rhs!, u0_nw, (0.0, 7*86400.0), p_no_walls), Tsit5(); reltol=1e-6, saveat=3600.0)

Impact of walls

MetricRoof onlyRoof + wallsEnergyPlus
Q_heat mean46 W/m²106 W/m²94 W/m²
Energy (7 d)7.7 kWh/m²17.7 kWh/m²15.8 kWh/m²
Error vs EP−52%+12%ref
U_eff4.911.510.2 W/(m²·K)

Without walls, the model misses more than half the heat losses. Adding them brings the result within 12% of EnergyPlus.

Why +12% and not 0%?

The residual overestimation comes from the uniform sky view factor FF_sky = 0.5 applied to all walls. In reality, gable walls partially face the ground and neighbouring surfaces, seeing less sky. EnergyPlus computes exact geometric view factors per surface.

Custom wall view factor

You can build a GreenhouseParams directly if you need finer control:

p_custom = GreenhouseParams(
    25.0,       # A floor
    0.3,        # ϕ roof slope
    3.08,       # h_air
    # ... cover, air, floor capacities ...
    50.8,       # A_walls
    2600*840*1e-3*50.8,  # C_wall
    0.35,       # FF_wall_sky, reduced view factor
    # ... heating params ...
)
